metadata.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP2 | D2.4: Connecting and sharing pan-genome information across different plant platforms
metadata.description: This deliverable (D2.4) reports on the prototyping of data formats, development of API calls, and the subsequent integration and visualization of pan-genome data across established plant platforms. Led by ELIXIR UK with assistance from ELIXIR Belgium, this work was conducted under Work Package 3 (WP3) of the E-PAN project within the BFSP Commissioned Service, specifically linking the PLAZA/FRINGE comparative genomics environment with the KnetMiner knowledge graph.

contributors.name: Heuermann, Marc Christian
contributors.affiliation: Leibniz Institute of Plant Genetics and Crop Plant Research

grants.title: European Life-science Infrastructure for Biological Information

data.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP2 | D2.4: Connecting and sharing pan-genome information across different plant platforms

metadata.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP2 | D 2.3: Prototyping and evaluation of data visualisations to represent pan-genome dynamics
metadata.description: This deliverable (D2.3) reports on the prototyping and evaluation of data visualizations for representing pan-genome dynamics, as specified in Activity 2 (T2.2) of WP2 (E-PAN) under the BFSP Commissioned Service. Building on T2.1's tool for presence/absence variation (PAV) detection, the prototypes enable exploration of structural and proteomic variation in plant pan-genomes, through web-based visualization tools. The advancements come from two parallel developments: the first within the German node (IPK and FZJ), the second within the Belgian node (VIB).

metadata.title: Annotation of inverted repeats displaying features of pble STIR or IR in the hg38, mm10, galGal5 and dr11 genome models
metadata.description: Annotation of inverted repeats displaying features of pble STIR or IR in the hg38 genome model. The annotation of pble-like inner inverted repeats was done using Palindrome (EMBOSS package). The output file was then filtered using pal2gff (https://github.com/Leelouh/pal2gff/blob/main/pal2gff.py), using as parameters a repeat size between 5 and 15 nucleotides, a spacer between pairs of inverted repeats (IRs) of 2 to 10 nucleotides, and a number of mismatches within repeats ranging from 0 to 1. These parameters were chosen taking into account those of the inner IRs found at ends of invertebrate pbles.

data.title: Annotation of inverted repeats displaying features of pble STIR or IR in the hg38, mm10, galGal5 and dr11 genome models

metadata.title: Supplemental Data for ExoLabel
metadata.description:
# Files in this upload

## Analyses

See the associated GitHub repository for an explanation of these files.

### RuntimeBenchmark.tar.gz

This archive contains the results of the runtime benchmark, in ExoBenchResults.RData.

### RealDataBenchmark.tar.gz

This archive contains data used for the real data benchmark in Figure 2 and the supplemental figures of the main text. Source data are contained in SyntheticBenchmarkSourceGraphs.tar.gz

- 50GenomeSetBlast_v5.RData: Results from the 50 genome benchmark
- 100GenomeSetBlast_v5.RData: Results from the 100 genome benchmark
- 200GenomeSetBlast_v5.RData: Results from the 200 genome benchmark
- 400GenomeSetBlast_v5.RData: Results from the 400 genome benchmark

### SyntheticBenchmark.tar.gz

This archive contains data used for the synthetic LFR graph benchmark in the supplemental information. The synthetic LFR graphs themselves are are stored in SyntheticBenchmarkSourceGraphs.tar.gz.

- SyntheticResults.RData: Results of running algorithms on various LFR graphs

## Raw Data

### SyntheticDataSourceGraphs.tar.gz

**~120GB uncompressed**

This archive contains all source LFR graphs used in the supplemental synthetic data benchmark, stored as RData files.

### RealBenchmarkSourceData.tar.gz

**~90GB uncompressed**

This archive contains all the disjoint genome sets used in the real data benchmark, stored as TSV files.

### FullPairwiseBlastScores.tar.gz

**~250GB uncompressed**

This archive contains the raw pairwise BLAST scores used for the real benchmark.

### Assemblies.tar.gz

**~10GB uncompressed**

This archive contains genome assemblies for each genome used in the benchmark.

metadata.title: Raw Oxford NanoPore MinION data (fast5 format) for Brand et al. 2026
metadata.description:
Raw Oxford NanoPore MinION data (fast5 format) for two fungal species (Isaria sinclairii and Mycelia sterilia), published in:

Brand et al. 2026: "Evolution, structure and function of the biosynthetic gene cluster of myriocin, a potent inhibitory sphingolipid" (https://doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.rs-6881806/v1).

All data is from one R9.4 flowcell (MIN-FLO106), prior to demultiplexing and basecalling. It contains the following four ONT barcodes (see barcodes.txt):

  • barcodes 05 and 07: Isaria sinclairii
  • barcodes 06 and 08: Mycelia sterilia

Barcodes 05 and 06 were from Ampure-XP size-selected libraries and comprise almost all the data. Barcodes 07 and 08 were BluePippin size-selected and comprise a small amount of additional data. In the original publication, the raw data were processed in ALBACORE 2.3.1 and PORECHOP 0.2.3 before genome assembly.

data.title: Raw Oxford NanoPore MinION data (fast5 format) for Brand et al. 2026

metadata.title: ver7 reference genome assembly of Spodoptera frugiperda
metadata.description:
This is a reference genome assembly and gene annotation of Spodoptera frugiperda, which was produced from corn strain. The original paper can be found at 10.1186/s12862-022-02090-x.

The name of this assembly is ver7, or simply v7. Kiwoong Nam, a researcher at INRAE, generated this assembly using PacBio and HiC data.

This genome assembly was made from PacBio reads, which was corrected by Illumina reads. The assembly was SMARTdenovo. Subsequently, scaffolding was performed using HiC with 3D DNA. Gap filling was performed using SOAP-denovo2 Gap-Closer and LR_GapCloser v1.1.

The gene annotation was performed using maker against the ver6.0 genome assembly. Detailed information can be found here: DOI: 10.1186/s12864-024-10845-7 This genome assembly was transferred to ver7 using RATT.

 

Pleas see doi: 10.1186/s12862-022-02090-x for detailed information.

data.title: ver7 reference genome assembly of Spodoptera frugiperda
